item.title: M&A activity to accelerate this year despite war disruption, Goldman Sachs says
item.description: Goldman Sachs expects mergers and acquisitions activity to be on the upswing this year despite the disruption caused by the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran, CEO ​David Solomon said on Friday.

item.title: Who hurts most as Iran war hits global economy?
item.description: Any prolongation of the Iran warrisks creating an unprecedented crisis in energy supplies that sooner or later will hit every corner of the global economy.

item.title: China's Xpeng posts first profit on high-margin EV sales
item.description: Chinese electric vehicle maker Xpeng reported its maiden quarterly profit, riding on strong sales of higher-margin models and lucrative technology partnerships.

item.title: Wall St futures slip as Iran war rages on, investors dial down rate cut bets
item.description: U.S. stock index futures slipped in choppy trading on Friday ​as the Iran war approached its fourth week, roiling energy markets and prompting investors to aggressively reprice ‌bets on interest rate cuts by the U.S. Federal Reserve.

item.title: Morning Bid: Battle of the barrel
item.description: Everything Mike Dolan and the ROI team are excited to read, watch and listen to over the weekend.

item.title: FedEx shares surge as investors cheer resilient demand, higher profit forecast
item.description: FedEx shares surged about 10% before the bell on Friday, after the package-delivery giant raised its full-year profit forecast and signaled steady ​shipping demand despite geopolitical tensions and surging fuel costs.

item.title: Germany eases arms exports restrictions for Gulf states, Ukraine
item.description: Germany has simplified the export of certain air and naval defence equipment ​to the Gulf states and Ukraine ‌to ensure deliveries arrive rapidly, said the economy ministry on Friday.

item.title: China's Xpeng to launch EVs for Latin American market
item.description: Chinese electric ‌vehicle maker Xpeng will launch EVs ​for the Latin ​American market at an ⁠event in ​Mexico on March ​25, it said on Friday.

item.title: UBS wealth management outflows threaten US turnaround, analysts and sources say
item.description: Swiss bank UBS Group is facing obstacles in revamping its U.S. wealth management business after losing billions of dollars in client assets and nearly 200 financial advisers, according to analysts and three industry sources ​with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Energy crisis pulls stocks 5% below January high, but path to quick recovery remains
item.description: Stock markets are facing their sharpest volatility in months as the S&P 500 is down more than 5% from its record ​high for the first time since November, leaving investors to weigh whether this represents a buying opportunity or the onset of a more severe ‌pullback.

item.title: Investors drive US money market fund assets to records as war-related risk fears multiply
item.description: As the Iran conflict intensifies, the spike in oil prices and rising inflation ​fears are spurring investors to ditch stocks as too risky and shun traditional safe havens such as gold in favor ‌of money market funds.

item.title: Rising gas prices hitting US household finances, Reuters/Ipsos poll finds
item.description: Rising gasoline prices are already starting to bite U.S. household ​finances and Americans overwhelmingly expect fuel costs will keep climbing as President Donald Trump's war with Iran crimps ‌global oil supplies, a new poll found.

item.title: US airlines lean on demand, fares as Iran war rattles overseas peers
item.description: U.S. airline chiefs are talking about the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran in a way that many of their counterparts overseas are not — projecting confidence about fares and demand even as fuel ​costs surge and global aviation is disrupted.

item.title: Wall Street banks with large trading units may be biggest winners under US capital plan
item.description: While all Wall Street banks are winners under a U.S. proposal to reduce the amount of capital they must hold, institutions with big trading operations stand to benefit more ​than traditional lenders, and this could potentially pit them against one another as they seek final revisions.

item.title: Powell's decision on Fed board seat could shape Warsh's leadership
item.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's upcoming decision on whether to keep his seat on the U.S. central bank's Board of Governors after his leadership term ends is now a key factor in how the tenure of his would-be successor Kevin Warsh evolves, and whether President Donald Trump and Treasury Secretary ​Scott Bessent can pursue any overhaul of the Fed's structure, operations and monetary policy.

item.title: Exclusive: Amazon plans smartphone comeback more than a decade after Fire Phone flop
item.description: In 2014, Amazon introduced its first smartphone, hoping to take on Apple and Samsung. Instead, the Fire Phone - overseen directly by founder Jeff Bezos - was scrapped in barely over a year, one of Amazon’s highest-profile flops.

item.title: US auto safety regulator closes defect petition on over 2 million Tesla vehicles
item.description: The U.S. auto safety regulator on Friday rejected a petition seeking ​a sweeping recall of about 2.26 million Tesla vehicles, saying ‌it had found no evidence of a safety-related defect.

item.title: Hyundai to recall over 61,000 Palisade SUVs in US after fatal incident, NHTSA says
item.description: Hyundai Motor is recalling 61,093 Palisade SUVs in the U.S. ​following a fatal accident linked ‌to a power seat issue, the U.S. National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) ​said on Friday.

item.title: Beijing-backed brain chip firm says it is three years behind Musk's Neuralink
item.description: Leading Chinese state-backed brain-computer interface (BCI) startup NeuCyber Neurotech said its ​most cutting-edge product is still three years behind Elon Musk's Neuralink, as Beijing races to expand clinical ‌trials.

item.title: Investors buy the dip in both stocks and bonds, BofA says
item.description: Investors took advantage of the drop in stock and bond ​prices in the latest week to ‌snap up both, even though a steep rise in energy prices on the back ​of the war in the Middle ​East rattled sentiment, according to Bank ⁠of America Global Research on Friday.

item.title: Unilever in takeover talks with McCormick for food business
item.description: Unilever said on Friday it was in talks ​with U.S.-based McCormick & Company about selling ‌its foods business, which includes brands such as Hellmann's mayonnaise and Knorr ​soups and seasonings.

item.title: War in Iran threatens fresh food-price shock across developing world
item.description: Disrupted fertiliser shipments and soaring energy prices from the war in Iran are threatening to unleash a fresh ​food-price surge across vulnerable nations, risking a years-long setback just as many were recovering from successive global shocks.

item.title: China's record oil output reaches limits of what's possible
item.description: China, the world's top oil importer, succeeded in a seven-year campaign to boost its own production, achieving a record high last year with aggressive drilling at ageing fields, an offshore boom and nascent shale oil output.

item.title: Novartis to buy experimental breast cancer drug in up to $3 billion deal
item.description: Swiss drugmaker Novartis on Friday agreed ​to buy a breast cancer drug candidate for up ‌to $3 billion from U.S. biotech firm Synnovation Therapeutics, adding a targeted therapy to its pipeline of cancer drugs.

item.title: WTO reform deadlock may prompt some countries to seek other options on free trade
item.description: Failure to chart a viable reform path for the World Trade Organization at a meeting next week will nudge members to pursue other options to set rules and advance ​free trade, diplomats and officials told Reuters.

item.title: Work from home, avoid air travel to deal with higher energy prices, IEA says
item.description: The International Energy Agency (IEA), which this month agreed a record release of oil from strategic stockpiles to deal ​with the effects of the U.S-Israeli war with ‌Iran, outlined on Friday proposals to ease oil price pressures on consumers, such as working-from-home and avoiding air travel.

item.title: Australia's Star Entertainment names Bally's executive Charles Diao as CFO
item.description: Australian casino operator Star Entertainment ​Group said on Friday it has appointed HC ‌Charles Diao as group chief financial officer, marking another leadership addition from U.S.-based Bally's Corporation .

item.title: Morning Bid: Hawkish rate repricing halts the dollar's rally
item.description: A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Rae Wee

item.title: Virgin Australia to adjust fares to reflect cost pressures
item.description: Virgin Australia is adjusting fares as rising costs across the aviation sector are "exacerbated by ​the situation in the Middle East", ‌the Australian carrier said in a statement to Reuters on Friday.

item.title: J.P. Morgan sees ECB hiking rates in April, July on rising inflation risks
item.description: J.P. Morgan on Friday said it ​expects the European ‌Central Bank (ECB) to deliver two rate hikes in ​April and July, ​in sharp contrast to ⁠its previous forecast ​of steady rates through ​2026, as policymakers signaled at rate hike discussions due ​to rising inflation ​risks.
